events.label: Supreme Court rules president cannot fire Federal Reserve governors (Trump v. Cook)

impacts.reason: Preserves central bank independence from political removal
Reinforces protections for independent agency heads across government

events.label: Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair but clashes with White House over rate cuts

impacts.reason: Warsh's hawkish 'sound money' orientation conflicts with Trump's demand for rate cuts, recreating Fed-White House tension
Markets whipsaw on conflicting signals about monetary policy direction

events.label: Warsh confirmation delayed past May, leaving Fed without confirmed chair

impacts.reason: Leadership vacuum at the Federal Reserve during period of economic stress
Unprecedented gap in central bank leadership undermines institutional credibility

events.label: Congress passes Federal Reserve Independence Act with veto-proof majority

impacts.reason: Codifies Fed independence into law
Signals bipartisan commitment to institutional norms

events.label: Courts strike down Section 122 tariffs as lacking legal basis

impacts.reason: Removes tariffs and further constrains unilateral presidential trade authority
Courts continue enforcing statutory limits on executive action

events.label: Section 122 tariffs expire July 24 with no congressional replacement

impacts.reason: Tariffs drop to zero, easing trade friction but creating policy whiplash
Binary outcome creates massive uncertainty for businesses planning around tariff levels
Loss of tariff revenue blows hole in federal budget projections

events.label: Congress grants new standalone tariff authority to the president

impacts.reason: Codifies broad tariff power that courts have been constraining
Legitimizes unpredictable unilateral trade policy

events.label: Major tariff exemptions granted to allied nations under Section 122

impacts.reason: Reduces trade tensions with allies
Signals more predictable trade policy direction

events.label: Courts order $200B+ in IEEPA tariff refunds, creating fiscal crisis

impacts.reason: Massive unplanned refund obligation strains federal budget
Deters future unilateral tariff actions

events.label: Executive order declares national emergency over election integrity, mandating hand-counting and re-registration

impacts.reason: Creates chaos in election administration months before midterms
Bypasses congressional authority over election law
Triggers nationwide protests and deepens partisan divide

events.label: SAVE Act proof-of-citizenship requirement filibustered in Senate

impacts.reason: Status quo preserved but becomes campaign wedge for election-fraud narrative
Fuels claims that elections are 'insecure,' laying groundwork for post-election challenges

events.label: Multiple states enact new mail-voting and early-voting restrictions before November

impacts.reason: Patchwork of new rules creates voter confusion and suppresses turnout

events.label: New Voting Rights Act passes with preclearance requirements

impacts.reason: Restores federal oversight of election law changes
Strengthens constitutional voting protections

events.label: Federal court issues injunction blocking Schedule F mass firings

impacts.reason: Temporarily halts reclassification of career civil servants
Courts assert limits on executive authority over civil service

events.label: First wave of Schedule F firings targets 10,000+ policy-adjacent career staff

impacts.reason: Accelerates loss of institutional knowledge and independent expertise
Agencies lose capacity to implement or enforce regulations consistently
Intelligence and defense agencies lose experienced personnel

events.label: Court orders reinstatement of fired inspectors general

impacts.reason: Restores independent oversight capacity
Affirms limits on executive power

events.label: Officials held in contempt of court receive presidential pardons, neutralizing judicial enforcement

impacts.reason: Effectively eliminates the judiciary's ability to enforce orders against the executive
Destroys the constitutional check of judicial review

events.label: Congress passes legislation limiting federal court jurisdiction over executive actions

impacts.reason: Removes judicial oversight of executive branch actions
Fundamentally alters separation of powers
Eliminates primary legal avenue for challenging government overreach

events.label: House moves to impeach federal judges who ruled against administration

impacts.reason: Creates chilling effect on judicial independence
Further politicizes the judiciary

events.label: DOJ establishes formal barriers between White House and prosecutorial decisions

impacts.reason: Insulates prosecution from political interference
Strengthens norms of independent enforcement

events.label: Insurrection Act invoked to deploy military against domestic protests

impacts.reason: Military deployed for domestic political purposes
Military force used against civilian protesters
Bypasses Posse Comitatus limits on domestic military use

events.label: Foreign interference in 2026 midterms succeeds due to degraded CISA defenses

impacts.reason: CISA at 38% staffing with election security program eliminated
Foreign actors exploit gap in cyber defenses to interfere with election infrastructure

events.label: Venezuela intervention becomes sustained military commitment requiring additional deployments

impacts.reason: Military stretched across domestic and foreign commitments without congressional authorization
Unbudgeted military operations strain federal spending

events.label: Intelligence Authorization Act strengthens analyst independence protections

impacts.reason: Creates statutory protections for analytic objectivity

events.label: DOJ brings Espionage Act charges against journalist for publishing classified leaks

impacts.reason: Criminalizes core investigative journalism using national security statutes
Chills whistleblowing and public access to government information

events.label: FTC/DOJ pretextual investigations used to coerce or shut down critical media outlets

impacts.reason: Regulatory agencies weaponized as tools of press suppression
Demonstrates government willingness to use regulatory power for political retaliation

events.label: Federal press shield law enacted protecting journalist sources

impacts.reason: Provides legal protection for investigative journalism

events.label: Bipartisan procurement reform bill strengthens competitive bidding requirements

impacts.reason: Reduces opportunities for political favoritism
Strengthens merit-based government operations

events.label: DOGE official's conflicts of interest trigger congressional investigation or criminal referral

impacts.reason: Increased scrutiny slows politically-motivated contract decisions
Accountability mechanisms activated

events.label: Direct confrontation between federal agents and state/local law enforcement over immigration enforcement

impacts.reason: Federal-state enforcement conflict risks violence and constitutional crisis
Federalism breakdown as competing enforcement authorities clash
Domestic law enforcement resources diverted to intergovernmental conflict

events.label: Politically motivated attack on government official or facility

impacts.reason: Escalates political violence trajectory
May trigger restrictions on rhetoric and assembly

events.label: Democrats win House majority in 2026 midterms, establishing congressional check

impacts.reason: Restores congressional oversight, subpoena power, and funding leverage
Enables legislative checks on executive overreach
Divided government historically constrains radical policy shifts

events.label: Bipartisan commission on democratic norms issues consensus recommendations

impacts.reason: Models cross-partisan cooperation
Creates roadmap for institutional reforms
